compiled_state.title: Move rebar writable state out of .claude/ (scratch base + migration stamps) to .rebar/
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
rebar still uses the Claude-Code plugin home .claude/ as its writable state root — dso-era residue. The scratch subcommand silently litters .claude/scratch/ in any host repo, and migration stamps land in .claude/ too, while rebar's actual config root is .rebar/ (read-config.sh). Behavior-visible change: land in the breaking window (clean break, no old-path fallback, per audit decision).

## What
- Default scratch base: REPO_ROOT/.claude/scratch -> REPO_ROOT/.rebar/scratch. Sites: ticket-scratch-set.sh:21, ticket-scratch-get.sh, ticket-scratch-clear.sh:50, helpers ticket-lib.sh:1694-1820, fallback read ticket-show.sh:166. Keep honoring the SCRATCH_BASE_DIR override.
- Migration stamp files .claude/.{closure-checks,file-impact}-migration-v1 -> .rebar/. Sites: ticket-migrate-closure-checks-v1.sh:65, ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh:66.
- Update asserting tests: test-ticket-migrate-closure-checks-v1.sh:254,272,371,428; test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh:185,237; scratch suites.
- TDD: flip the path assertions first (RED), then move the defaults (GREEN).

## Scope
Path defaults only; scratch/migration semantics unchanged. No back-compat shim for the old paths (documented in release notes).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No rebar code writes under .claude/ (git grep '\.claude' src/ clean)
- [ ] SCRATCH_BASE_DIR override still honored; docs mention the new default
- [ ] Old-path fallback NOT added (clean break); change noted in release notes
- [ ] Full suite green

comments.body:
EXECUTOR CORRECTIONS + DECISIONS (from naive-executor dry-run, 2026-06-09):
1. LINE REFS (verified): scratch helpers using .claude/scratch are at ticket-lib.sh:1735 and :1820 (function block ~1706-1880, not 1694-1820 as the description says). ticket-show.sh's fallback is at :173 inside an EMBEDDED PYTHON block (lines ~164-193) — edit the Python heredoc, not shell.
2. SCRATCH_BASE_DIR: the override is an optional absolute path; ONLY the empty-default changes (.claude/scratch -> .rebar/scratch). Override handling code unchanged.
3. TEST INVENTORY (complete): test-ticket-migrate-closure-checks-v1.sh (path assertions at :17,:254,:268,:272,:297,:352,:371,:401,:428), test-ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh (:185,:237), plus ALL scratch suites: grep -l scratch tests/scripts/ and tests/test-reconciler-scratch-exclude.sh — update path expectations in each.
4. MIGRATION-STAMP IDEMPOTENCY DECISION: moving the stamp would silently RE-RUN the kept migrations on stores that already ran them (old stamp at .claude/ unchecked). Decision: migration scripts CHECK BOTH locations read-only (old .claude/ stamp counts as already-migrated) but WRITE only the new .rebar/ stamp. This is the single deliberate exception to the no-old-path-fallback rule; scratch itself gets NO fallback (clean break stands). Add a test: old-location stamp present -> migration is a no-op.
5. No warning needed when SCRATCH_BASE_DIR points at .claude/scratch explicitly — explicit override is the user's choice.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Done. Moved rebar writable-state defaults .claude/ -> .rebar/: scratch base in ticket-lib.sh (_scratch helpers used by scratch set/get), ticket-scratch-clear.sh, and the ticket_reads.py fallback read; migration stamps in ticket-migrate-{closure-checks,file-impact}-v1.sh -> .rebar/. Updated the asserting migration tests and all doc-comments. SCRATCH_BASE_DIR override preserved. README now documents the .rebar/scratch default + override. No old-path fallback added (clean break). Also migrated the next-batch dir_roots heuristic .claude -> .rebar.

DOCUMENTED RESIDUE: one .claude literal remains in src — ticket-migrate-file-impact-v1.sh:53, the legacy plugin-source-repo guard checking $_PLUGIN_ROOT/.claude-plugin/plugin.json. That manifest no longer exists anywhere in standalone rebar, so the guard is inert (can never fire). Left as-is: it is not a writable-state path and rewriting the migration guard's self-skip logic is beyond this path-migration story (candidate for dedicated dso-residue cleanup). Functional smoke confirmed scratch now writes/reads under .rebar/scratch with no .claude created. Full suite green (1311).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
